steps.text: A confidence interval built from a sample mean and margin of error defines the range of plausible values for the true population mean. The interval spans from (mean − margin of error) to (mean + margin of error).
Given: sample mean = $31.86$ ounces, margin of error = $0.18$ ounce. Lower bound: $31.86 - 0.18 = 31.68$ ounces. Upper bound: $31.86 + 0.18 = 32.04$ ounces.

The interpretation is that plausible values for the true average weight of all bags lie between $31.68$ ounces and $32.04$ ounces. This does not say the true mean IS in the interval with certainty, only that these are plausible values consistent with the sample.
The correct answer states plausible values are between $31.68$ and $32.04$ ounces, which exactly matches our calculation. That confirms A is correct.

choices.text: • Plausible values between $31.68$ and $32.04$ ounces matches mean ± margin of error: $31.86 \pm 0.18$. That confirms A is correct.
• This says plausible values are OUTSIDE the interval — the opposite of what a margin of error means. Eliminated.
• This says the mean is less than $31.99$ ounces. But $31.99$ has no special significance here, and margin of error defines an interval around the estimate, not a one-sided bound. Eliminated.
• This says the mean is greater than $31.99$ ounces. The upper bound is $32.04$, not $31.99$, and again the margin of error defines a two-sided interval. Eliminated.
